evidence_refs.source_quote: Replace one general travel lane in each direction and maintain curb access

rationale: Source is the 3rd public workshop for 125th Street SBS; offset bus lanes presented as a core SBS feature on the corridor.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Replace one general travel lane in each direction and maintain curb access

quarantine.note: Cited blocks p002_c0002 ('Offset bus lanes') and p002_c0003 ('Replace one general travel lane in each direction and maintain curb access') are generic SBS feature descriptions from page 2's 'Features of Select Bus Service' overview. Neither block mentions 125th Street, the project name, or the corridor. The '+selectbusservice 125th Street' text exists only in a separate block (order 39) on the same page — this is pure document-level co-occurrence, not a statement of connection between the two records.

rationale: Off-board fare collection is listed as a Select Bus Service feature in the 125th Street SBS workshop #3 presentation.

quarantine.note: Cited blocks p002_c0006 ('Off-board fare collection') and p002_c0007 ('Reduces wait time at stations') are generic SBS feature descriptions from page 2. Neither mentions 125th Street or the project. Co-occurrence with a separate footer block on the same page is not a relation.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Reduces bus lane blockages and increases bus speeds

rationale: Bus lane enforcement is listed as a SBS feature for the 125th Street corridor in workshop #3.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Reduces bus lane blockages and increases bus speeds

quarantine.note: Cited blocks p002_c0008 ('Bus lane enforcement') and p002_c0009 ('Reduces bus lane blockages and increases bus speeds') are generic SBS feature descriptions from page 2. Neither mentions 125th Street or the project. Co-occurrence is not a relation.

rationale: Transit signal priority is shown as a Select Bus Service feature for 125th Street in the workshop #3 presentation.

quarantine.note: Two problems: (1) Cited blocks p002_c0014 ('Transit signal priority') and p002_c0015 ('Reduces stop time at intersections') are generic SBS feature descriptions from page 2 — neither mentions 125th Street. Co-occurrence is not a relation. (2) The base_record_id is 'treatment_transit-signal-priority-125th' (source 2013_03_13_sbs_125th_cb10) but the proposed object_id is 'treatment_transit-signal-priority-125th_2' (source 2013_04_09_sbs_125th_workshop_3) — these are different treatment records with different source_ids, and no merge has occurred.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Banning left turns reduces turning conflicts and keeps through traffic moving more smoothly

rationale: Left-turn bans are proposed as a traffic improvement on 125th Street in the SBS workshop #3 presentation.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Banning left turns reduces turning conflicts and keeps through traffic moving more smoothly

quarantine.note: Cited block p013_c0008 ('Banning left turns reduces turning conflicts and keeps through traffic moving more smoothly') appears on page 13 under 'Traffic Analysis > Potential Improvements' but does not itself mention 125th Street, the project, or the corridor. The page footer containing '+selectbusservice 125th Street' is a separate block. Co-occurrence is not a relation.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Right-turn Bays: reduce blockages at intersections

rationale: Right-turn bays are shown in the 125th Street proposed cross-section design (page 7) and on the segment maps (pages 15-16). They reduce intersection blockages on the corridor.

quarantine.note: The base_record_id is 'treatment_right-turn-bays-125th' (source 2013_03_13_sbs_125th_cb10) but the proposed object_id is 'treatment_right-turn-bays-125th_2' (source 2013_04_09_sbs_125th_workshop_3) — these are different treatment records with different source_ids and different locations payloads. No merge has occurred between these records. Additionally, the evidence_refs at the decision level cite p015_c0003 while the proposed_relations evidence_refs cite p007_c0002 — inconsistent evidence chains.
